channel.title: Daily News Digest
channel.description: Curated daily news, filtered by Claude

item.title: Ebola outbreak may be spreading faster than first thought, WHO doctor warns
item.description:
Hundreds of cases are suspected in central Africa but experts fear the actual number may be much higher.

Large-scale Ebola outbreak in central Africa with hundreds of suspected cases represents a significant public health crisis that informed people should know about.

item.title: Trump says he called off new Iran attack at request of Gulf states
item.description:
The US president says he is holding off on an attack planned for Tuesday as "serious negotiations are now taking place".

Trump's decision to call off a planned Iran attack due to ongoing negotiations is a major geopolitical shift affecting US-Iran relations.

item.title: What really holds China and Russia together
item.description:
The relationship endures despite a power imbalance, with both sides recognising that it is too important to fail.

Analysis of China-Russia relationship endurance is relevant geopolitical context for understanding major power dynamics.

item.title: Trump told Taiwan not to 'go independent' - but does it want to?
item.description:
Taiwan independence is the reddest of red lines for China, which claims the island as its own.

Trump's statement about Taiwan independence is a significant geopolitical development touching on a major US-China flashpoint.

item.title: WHO official warns Ebola outbreak unlikely to be over in two months as cases and deaths rise in DRC
item.description:
At least 130 people thought to have been killed, says Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus before emergency meeting‘It’s heartbreaking’: panic in eastern DRC over return of EbolaThe director general of the World Health Organization has said he is deeply concerned about the scale and the speed of the Ebola out

WHO warning that Ebola outbreak in DRC will last longer than two months with rising deaths confirms scale of major public health crisis.

item.title: Pressure on Mexico after two ex-officials surrender to US over alleged cartel ties
item.description:
Mexican president Claudia Sheinbaum denies any links between her Morena party and organized crimePressure is mounting on Claudia Sheinbaum, Mexico’s president, after two former top officials from the country’s Sinaloa state – both members of her Morena party – gave themselves up to US authorities ov

Mexican officials surrendering to US over cartel ties represents a significant development in Mexico's organized crime and governance.

item.title: Drones reshape war in Colombia as deaths and injuries mount
item.description:
Civilians left increasingly exposed as a dangerous new front opens up in the country’s decades-old conflictAs night fell over southern Colombia, and a group of children began their weekly Tuesday football match, a drone appeared overhead.The children looked up, and the drone dropped a grenade, its b

Drones reshaping Colombia's conflict with increasing civilian casualties represents a significant tactical shift in the country's decades-old war.

item.title: Fears of new China shock as EU industry’s reliance on imports grows
item.description:
Rising volume of components imported from China prompts warning of cannibalisation of European industriesEurope is facing a fresh China shock that threatens to cannibalise local factories, leading to job losses and de facto colonisation of industry by Beijing, trade analysts and representatives have

EU dependency on Chinese industrial imports creating vulnerability is a major economic and trade development affecting global supply chains.

item.title: Trump’s shifting remarks on Taiwan are perfect for China to exploit
item.description:
President’s remarks about Taiwan arms deals being a ‘negotiating chip’ with Beijing have been seized on by Chinese state mediaIt has been an unsettling few days for Taiwan’s government. When Donald Trump met Xi Jinping in Beijing on Thursday, many feared the unpredictable US leader could upend Washi

Major geopolitical shift: Trump's remarks on Taiwan as a negotiating chip signal potential US policy change on a critical flashpoint between US, China, and Taiwan.

item.title: Xi prepares to welcome Putin to China four days after hosting Trump
item.description:
Chinese state media say Beijing emerging as ‘focal point of global diplomacy’ with Russian leader arriving on TuesdayXi Jinping and Vladimir Putin exchanged “congratulatory letters” on Sunday ahead of the Russian president’s visit to Beijing this week, four days after Donald Trump left China after a

Strategic geopolitical moment: Xi hosting both Trump and Putin in succession demonstrates China positioning itself as central power broker amid US-Russia-China tensions.

item.title: Australian taxpayers to pay $11bn to extend lifespan of ageing Collins-class submarines amid Aukus delay
item.description:
HMAS Farncomb had been due to be retired this year, but is now expected to operate until about 2036Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastTaxpayers will fork out an extra $11bn to extend the lifespan of Australia’s ageing Coll

Major strategic decision: $11bn commitment to extend aging Collins-class submarines reflects Australia's long-term military posture amid Indo-Pacific tensions and AUKUS delays.

item.title: Middle East crisis live: Iran warns it will ‘open new fronts’ against US if attacks resume after Trump suspends strikes
item.description:
The US president said he called off a planned attack on Iran on Tuesday so that peace talks could continueFull report: Trump claims planned attack on Iran postponed after Tehran makes new proposal to end warIran’s army has warned it would “open new fronts” against the US if it resumes attacks on the

Major escalation risk: Iran warns of 'new fronts' against US, indicating potential for significant Middle East conflict expansion if negotiations fail.

item.title: Trump claims planned attack on Iran postponed after Tehran makes new proposal to end war
item.description:
The US president says plans for a US military strike on Iran have been paused because ‘serious negotiations are now taking place’Iran has made a new proposal for a deal to definitively end the war in the Middle East, officials in the region said on Monday, with Donald Trump claiming he had postponed

Strategic shift in Middle East conflict: Trump pauses Iran strike and claims new peace proposal, potentially signaling major change in US approach to Iran tensions.

item.title: WHO to hold emergency committee meeting as Ebola death toll rises to 131
item.description:
A panel of experts ⁠led by ⁠the WHO is to meet to discuss vaccine ⁠options for new epidemic.

Major public health concern: WHO emergency committee on Ebola with 131 deaths signals potential epidemic requiring international response.

item.title: At least 10 dead as huge floods sweep southern and central China
item.description:
The affected area extends more than 1,000km (620 miles), according to Chinese meteorologists.

Large-scale natural disaster: Major floods across southern and central China affecting area over 1,000km with at least 10 deaths.

item.title: Lebanon and Syria reshape ties amid Israeli attacks and regional shifts
item.description:
Damascus and Beirut focus on sovereignty and cooperation, moving away from decades of Syrian dominance over Lebanon.

Major regional realignment: Lebanon and Syria reshaping ties amid Israeli attacks signals significant shift in Levantine geopolitics away from decades of Syrian dominance.

item.title: Iran war day 81: Trump delays attack; Tehran says won’t ‘surrender’
item.description:
US president says he held off a scheduled attack on Iran after pressure from Gulf allies as renewed talks gain momentum.

Major geopolitical shift: US delayed military strike on Iran and renewed talks suggest significant de-escalation in a potential major conflict.

item.title: Ebola outbreak: WHO declares emergency, US restricts travel, American infected
item.description:
CDC is working to move the infected American and six others to Germany.

Large-scale natural disaster/health emergency: WHO declares Ebola outbreak an emergency with US travel restrictions and infected American, meeting the natural disaster threshold.

item.title: A revolutionary cancer treatment could transform autoimmune disease
item.description:
Researchers are testing CAR T cell therapy as a way to reset the immune system.

Major scientific breakthrough: CAR T cell therapy being tested to reset immune system for autoimmune diseases represents a transformative medical advance.

item.title: Ontario touts $8.5B annual economic impact from Toronto Island airport expansion — but won't show the math
item.description:
The Ontario government says expending Billy Bishop airport could provide an $8.5-billion annual economic boost by 2050. But weeks after first touting that claim, neither the province nor the Toronto Port Authority, which owns the airport, has provided any evidence to support it. Several experts say

Major Toronto decision: Ontario government's Billy Bishop airport expansion with claimed $8.5B annual impact is a significant provincial/city infrastructure decision affecting Toronto.

item.title: Tentative deal struck between TTC, maintenance workers union: TTC CEO
item.description:
The Toronto Transit Commission and the union representing maintenance workers reached a tentative deal Monday afternoon, both parties said in statements.

Significant Toronto infrastructure event: TTC tentative deal with maintenance workers union resolves labor dispute affecting transit service for the city.

item.title: Storing Ontario's $79.1M stockpile of delisted U.S. alcohol could cost up to $20M a year
item.description:
Ontario could be spending $20 million a year to store $79.1 million in delisted U.S. alcohol products, according to one operations management expert. The liquor ban became one of Ontario's most visible responses to tariffs and annexation threats from U.S. President Donald Trump last year.

Major provincial policy decision directly affecting Toronto with significant financial and economic implications from the Ford government's tariff response.

item.title: TTC, maintenance workers union continue negotiations over 36 hours after initial deadline
item.description:
The Toronto Transit Commission and the union representing maintenance workers continued collective agreement negotiations into Sunday afternoon — over 36 hours past the initial Saturday deadline.

Significant infrastructure event affecting Toronto's transit system with potential city-wide impact.
